arts.ttl: ‘Work in progress’ - how Maresca is turning Chelsea around
arts.sum: Amid challenges of an oversized Chelsea squad and external criticism, Guillem Balague looks at how new manager Enzo Maresca has turned the club's fortunes around.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid’s TRIPLE injury blow, Man City RECORD defeat, Chelsea’s Kepa LOSS
arts.sum: Real Madrid ’s transfer plans for January have changed after they were rocked by a fresh injury blow, Manchester City have slipped further off the Premier League pace behind Liverpool and Chelsea are bracing themselves for a huge loss. As a result, Real Madrid are set to make moves in the January transfer window, with Fabrizio Romano confirming their interest in Trent Alexander-Arnold of Liverpool and Castello Lukeba of RB Leipzig.

arts.ttl: Chelsea star Noni Madueke sends Arsenal duo message over England 'bragging rights'
arts.sum: CHELSEA vs ARSENAL: Noni Madueke wants the Blues to sign off in style ahead of next week's international break when he will join forces with Gunners duo Bukayo Saka and Declan Rice on England duty

arts.ttl: Arsenal need to put away more chances, admits Leandro Trossard
arts.sum: Arteta’s side without league win since 5 October Arsenal visit Chelsea in London derby on Sunday Leandro Trossard has admitted Arsenal must become more ruthless if they want to turn around their fortunes against Chelsea. Mikel Arteta’s side have not won in the Premier League since 5 October and failed to score for the second game in succession in their defeat at Inter in the Champions League on Wednesday, when they registered 21 shots but only four on target. Trossard has been shouldering some of the creative responsibility in the absence of the captain Martin Ødegaard, who is expected to play some part at Stamford Bridge on Sunday after returning from injury against Inter. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Why Pep Guardiola proteges Mikel Arteta and Enzo Maresca will NOT replace him at Manchester City, writes DANNY MURPHY
arts.sum: It's a massive compliment to Pep Guardiola that two of the brightest Premier League managers worked for him at Manchester City and have greatly improved Chelsea and Arsenal on what they learned.

arts.ttl: THE SHARPE END: Arsenal are no longer marching forward but Martin Odegaard's return can lift the burden on Bukayo Saka
arts.sum: JAMES SHARPE: And now, at last, this was the season that the Gunners would take the final next step and win the title. Only they have gone backwards.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid receive new €250m offer for Chelsea target
arts.sum: Vinicius Junior continues to be linked with a move away from Real Madrid, and the latest club that is prepared to give him the option to do so is Paris Saint-Germain. Since missing out on the 2024 Ballon d’Or award to Manchester City midfielder Rodri Hernandez, Vinicius’ future at the Santiago Bernabeu has been the […] The post Real Madrid receive new €250m offer for Chelsea target appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Chelsea vs Arsenal: The results of the last 10 toxic London derbies
arts.sum: Analysing the last 10 meetings between Chelsea and Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Arsenal handed major pre-Chelsea boost as Premier League title race takes fresh twist
arts.sum: However, with City tipped to beat Brighton - despite form and injury concerns - Pep Guardiola could only watch on as his star-studded team let their one-goal advantage slip at the Amex Stadium to provide a welcome boost to their main title rivals. It was an eye-catching result which, no doubt, would have been celebrated loudly from the Emirates Stadium and beyond in what was the latest twist in an early-Premier League title race which has far from followed the script, so far.

arts.ttl: Cole Palmer injury return theory emerges as Chelsea star 'spotted' in training before Arsenal
arts.sum: He's getting better," he admitted in a press conference. Chelsea and Enzo Maresca are certainly keeping their cards close to their chest regarding Cole Palmer ahead of the Premier League clash against Arsenal.
